title: poa pratensis var. eragrostiformis
intro: Poa pratensis var. eragrostiformis, also known as the meadow grass or tufted hair grass, is a subspecies of the Kentucky bluegrass plant. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The plant's health benefits include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Poa pratensis var. eragrostiformis is often combined with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to support respiratory health, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation.
